clusters.title: Hero’s welcome for Omar Artan after Somali’s World Cup dreams dashed
clusters.neutral_headline: Hero’s welcome for Omar Artan after Somali’s World Cup dreams dashed
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, NPR News, France24 and others

items.title: Hero’s welcome for Omar Artan after Somali’s World Cup dreams dashed
items.standfirst: Referee Omar Artan was denied from the US, where he was set to become the first Somali to officiate at a FIFA World Cup.
items.content: Referee Omar Artan was denied from the US, where he was set to become the first Somali to officiate at a FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Somali World Cup referee denied U.S. entry, hailed as hero at home
items.standfirst: A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.
items.content: A World Cup dream denied — Somali referee Omar Artan receives a hero's welcome at home after being blocked from entering the United States and taking part in the World Cup.(Image credit: Abuukar Mohamed Muhidin)

items.title: M23 fighters detaining, abusing thousands in DRC
items.standfirst: In tonight's edition, a new Human Rights Watch report uncovers mass forced recruitment and detention by the Rwanda-backed M23 in eastern DR Congo. Also, we head to Durban where thousands of foreign nationals driven from their homes during xenophobic protests remain outside the Department of Home Affairs. And he’s back on home soil to a thunderous welcome, but Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan’s World Cup dream has been cut short after he was denied entry to the United States.
items.content: In tonight's edition, a new Human Rights Watch report uncovers mass forced recruitment and detention by the Rwanda-backed M23 in eastern DR Congo. Also, we head to Durban where thousands of foreign nationals driven from their homes during xenophobic protests remain outside the Department of Home Affairs. And he’s back on home soil to a thunderous welcome, but Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan’s World Cup dream has been cut short after he was denied entry to the United States.

items.title: Referee barred from entering U.S. for World Cup gets a hero’s welcome in Somalia
items.standfirst: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.
items.content: Somalia isn’t competing at the World Cup, but from the scenes at Mogadishu’s airport, you’d think the country had won the trophy.

items.title: US official claims banned referee had links with 'terror organisations'
items.standfirst: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations," says a US official.
items.content: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations," says a US official.

items.title: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup?
items.standfirst: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?
items.content: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?

items.title: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
items.standfirst: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".
items.content: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".

items.title: Trump says Iran has taken too long to negotiate, will 'pay the price'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. "Iran ‌is all talk and no action," Trump wrote in ⁠a social media post. "They’ve taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them, ‌now they will have to pay the price!!!"
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said Tehran has taken too ​long to negotiate a deal and would now "have to pay the price" after Iran and the United States exchanged strikes in the region amid reported efforts to continue ​talks. clusters.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
clusters.neutral_headline: Bill Gates tells US House ‘I have never victimized anyone’ in Epstein testimony
bullet_summary: One of the richest men in the world, Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, testified about his ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein on Wednesday
Gates said he should never have met Epstein and had no indication of ongoing criminal conduct
“I am here to answer your questions about my interactions with Jeffrey Epstein and to help contribute to the committee’s important work,” Gates said in his opening statement, seen by the Guardian

items.title: Bill Gates tells Congress that meeting Epstein was "a grave error in judgment"
items.standfirst: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."
items.content: Bill Gates told members of Congress on Wednesday that Jeffrey Epstein put his philanthropic work at risk, and that meeting him represented "a grave error in judgment."

items.title: Bill Gates faces hours of questioning over Jeffrey Epstein ties
items.standfirst: One of the richest men in the world, Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, testified about his ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein on Wednesday. Gates told lawmakers he never saw Epstein engage in criminal conduct. Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: One of the richest men in the world, Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, testified about his ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein on Wednesday. Gates told lawmakers he never saw Epstein engage in criminal conduct. Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: Bill Gates calls Epstein meetings a ‘grave error’ while defending actions in house testimony
items.standfirst: Bill Gates said on Wednesday he made a “grave error in judgment” by meeting Jeffrey Epstein but denied any wrongdoing, as the Microsoft co-founder faced hours of questioning before the House Oversight Committee about his ties to the disgraced financier. Gates said he should never have met Epstein and had no indication of ongoing criminal conduct.
items.content: Bill Gates said on Wednesday he made a “grave error in judgment” by meeting Jeffrey Epstein but denied any wrongdoing, as the Microsoft co-founder faced hours of questioning before the House Oversight Committee about his ties to the disgraced financier. Gates said he should never have met Epstein and had no indication of ongoing criminal conduct.

items.title: Bill Gates tells US House ‘I have never victimized anyone’ in Epstein testimony
items.standfirst: Microsoft co-founder appears in closed-door session as part of lawmakers’ investigation into convicted sex offenderBill Gates testified in front of the House committee on oversight and reform on Wednesday, and told lawmakers in his opening remarks that he “never witnessed nor had any indication” that Jeffrey Epstein was “engaged in ongoing criminal conduct”.“I am here to answer your questions about my interactions with Jeffrey Epstein and to help contribute to the committee’s important work,” Gates said in his opening statement, seen by the Guardian. “I support the release of all the Epstein files and sincerely hope that, through your efforts and those of others advocating on their behalf, the survivors of Epstein’s crimes can get the justice that they deserve.” Continue reading...
items.content: Microsoft co-founder appears in closed-door session as part of lawmakers’ investigation into convicted sex offenderBill Gates testified in front of the House committee on oversight and reform on Wednesday, and told lawmakers in his opening remarks that he “never witnessed nor had any indication” that Jeffrey Epstein was “engaged in ongoing criminal conduct”.“I am here to answer your questions about my interactions with Jeffrey Epstein and to help contribute to the committee’s important work,” Gates said in his opening statement, seen by the Guardian. “I support the release of all the Epstein files and sincerely hope that, through your efforts and those of others advocating on their behalf, the survivors of Epstein’s crimes can get the justice that they deserve.” Continue reading...

items.title: Bill Gates testifies in closed-door US House meeting over Epstein ties
items.standfirst: In prepared remarks, Gates told the House Oversight Committee that meeting Epstein was a 'grave error in judgment'.
items.content: In prepared remarks, Gates told the House Oversight Committee that meeting Epstein was a 'grave error in judgment'.

items.title: French star Patrick Bruel charged with rape and sexual assault
items.standfirst: Singer and actor has denied all charges after more than 20 women came forward with allegations going back to the 1990sThe French singer Patrick Bruel has been charged with rape and sexual assault in one of the biggest #MeToo cases in the French music industry.The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture, was placed under formal investigation over several cases that included alleged rape, attempted rape, sexual assault and sexual harassment. Continue reading...
items.content: Singer and actor has denied all charges after more than 20 women came forward with allegations going back to the 1990sThe French singer Patrick Bruel has been charged with rape and sexual assault in one of the biggest #MeToo cases in the French music industry.The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture, was placed under formal investigation over several cases that included alleged rape, attempted rape, sexual assault and sexual harassment. Continue reading...

items.title: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape charges
items.standfirst: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.
items.content: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.

items.title: Has France failed its children? Outrage over murder of 11-year old Lyhanna
items.standfirst: Here in France, outrage. Outrage over what seems like the all-too-avoidable murder of 11-year old Lyhanna. The 41-year old suspect in her May 29th disappearance in a southwestern town - the father of a schoolmate - had twice previously been flagged and never questioned about suspected paedophile crimes. We’ll ask what went wrong and about a string of high profile cases that have recently shaken the nation, from the trial of a doctor in western France who abused patients over a period of decades and a new mayor of Paris - himself a past victim of child sexual abuse - who’s suspended dozens of after-school staff who were the subjects of complaints, to formal charges against French pop icon Patrick Bruel this Wednesday over alleged rape and abuse dating back to 1997.
items.content: Here in France, outrage. Outrage over what seems like the all-too-avoidable murder of 11-year old Lyhanna. The 41-year old suspect in her May 29th disappearance in a southwestern town - the father of a schoolmate - had twice previously been flagged and never questioned about suspected paedophile crimes. We’ll ask what went wrong and about a string of high profile cases that have recently shaken the nation, from the trial of a doctor in western France who abused patients over a period of decades and a new mayor of Paris - himself a past victim of child sexual abuse - who’s suspended dozens of after-school staff who were the subjects of complaints, to formal charges against French pop icon Patrick Bruel this Wednesday over alleged rape and abuse dating back to 1997.

items.title: France prosecutors want singer Bruel charged over alleged sexual abuse
items.standfirst: French prosecutors said Wednesday they had requested pop icon Patrick Bruel be charged and placed in pre-trial detention over nine allegations of sexual abuse including rape and sexual assault. The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture with multiple top selling albums and dozens of film appearances, has denied any wrongdoing. He is the latest top French celebrity to face investigators in the wake of the #MeToo movement, after film legend Gerard Depardieu was handed a suspended 18-month prison sentence last year for sexually assaulting two women on a film set. FRANCE 24's James André reports from the courthouse near Paris.
items.content: French prosecutors said Wednesday they had requested pop icon Patrick Bruel be charged and placed in pre-trial detention over nine allegations of sexual abuse including rape and sexual assault. The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture with multiple top selling albums and dozens of film appearances, has denied any wrongdoing. He is the latest top French celebrity to face investigators in the wake of the #MeToo movement, after film legend Gerard Depardieu was handed a suspended 18-month prison sentence last year for sexually assaulting two women on a film set. FRANCE 24's James André reports from the courthouse near Paris.

items.title: India summons US envoy over attack on ship carrying Indian sailors off Oman
items.standfirst: Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attack on the Palau-flagged Settebello.
items.content: Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attack on the Palau-flagged Settebello.

items.title: Three Indian crew missing as US says fired on tanker off Oman
items.standfirst: Three Indian crew members were reported missing after a vessel was attacked off Oman, India said Wednesday, before the US military announced it had fired on a tanker attempting to breach its blockade of Iran. In a statement, the Indian foreign ministry condemned the attack on the vessel, the Settebello, saying: "Of the 24 Indian crew onboard, 21 Indians have been rescued thus far and three Indians are reportedly missing". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more.
items.content: Three Indian crew members were reported missing after a vessel was attacked off Oman, India said Wednesday, before the US military announced it had fired on a tanker attempting to breach its blockade of Iran. In a statement, the Indian foreign ministry condemned the attack on the vessel, the Settebello, saying: "Of the 24 Indian crew onboard, 21 Indians have been rescued thus far and three Indians are reportedly missing". FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more.

items.title: World’s largest, deepest, oldest whale graveyard discovered in Indian Ocean
items.standfirst: Chinese scientists have discovered a massive whale graveyard of around 500 skeletons, some dating back to more than 5 million years, at the bottom of the Indian Ocean west of Australia, according to research published in the journal Nature on Wednesday. The whale fall provides shelter and sustenance to a vibrant ecosystem of marine species, many believed to be new to science.
items.content: Chinese scientists have discovered a massive whale graveyard of around 500 skeletons, some dating back to more than 5 million years, at the bottom of the Indian Ocean west of Australia, according to research published in the journal Nature on Wednesday. The whale fall provides shelter and sustenance to a vibrant ecosystem of marine species, many believed to be new to science.
